nodes.name: Categoriser

options.systemMessage:
=<persona>
You are an email processing assistant.
</persona>
<task>
Look at the content of the email and decide whether to apply a label or archive it, processing it only once. First, archive those that are absolutely unnecessary using the mail_archiver tool. This judgment is the top priority. After that, if it does not fall into that category, determine whether it should be read based on the following criteria and use the mail_label_setter tool to apply the label.
<case>Absolutely unnecessary: Archive using the mail_archiver tool</case>
<case>Needs to be read: Apply "Label_[label_id]" using the mail_label_setter tool</case>
<case>Other: Apply "Label_[label_id]" using the mail_label_setter tool</case>
Report the processing results carefully.
</task>
<rules>
<Archive>
Emails that are absolutely unnecessary and will be archived
<item>[list up your rule1]</item>
<item>[list up your rule2]</item>
</Archive><MustRead>
Emails that need to be read
<item>[list up your rule1]</item>
<item>[list up your rule2]</item>
</MustRead>
<Other>
Emails that are not necessary to read but will not be archived
<item>[list up your rule1]</item>
<item>[list up your rule2]</item>
</Other>
</rules>

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content:
## Mail Agent
For emails in the inbox, archive those that are completely unnecessary, and label the rest based on their relevance.

### Trigger
Run by communicating with Telegram

### Get Mail via Gmail
Retrieve all emails in the Gmail inbox.
(Inbox = Label: INBOX)

### Filter
Filter out emails that have already been processed to avoid unnecessary work for the AI.

### AI Agent
Check each email one by one, categorize them as necessary or unnecessary according to the provided prompt, and instruct Gmail to apply the appropriate labels.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Router  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Verify the settings of the trigger node "Telegram Trigger".
Open the "OpenRouter Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "mail_label_setter" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "mail_archiver" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Aggregate" node (n8n-nodes-base.aggregate) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Telegram" node (n8n-nodes-base.telegram) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenRouter Chat Model1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Reporter" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get mails in INBOX" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Filter processed" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Categoriser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
